§6DFC Components & Operation§f

§aThe modular components of the DFC — how each part works and how to operate safely!§f

The DFC requires several specialized components in addition to the core. All are forged in the §bPlasma Forge§f with welded osmiridium plates and maximum plasma input energy.

§6Components§f:

§bCore§f (hbm:dfc_core) — where the reaction occurs. Holds 2 fuel types and a catalyst. GUI shows 4 indicators: fuel 1 type, fuel 2 type, restriction field strength, and heat saturation. If restriction reaches §e0%§f or heat reaches §e100%§f, the DFC explodes violently. The core receives an energy beam from the emitter.

§bEmitter§f (hbm:dfc_emitter) — supplies energy to trigger the reaction. §cMUST be cooled with Cryogel§f or it melts into lava. Output adjustable §e1-100%§f. Chained emitters have §e95%§f efficiency per chain.

§bReceiver§f (hbm:dfc_receiver) — absorbs generated energy, outputs electricity. Must point toward the beam arrival direction. §cRequires Cryogel cooling§f.

§bStabilizer§f (hbm:dfc_stabilizer) — contains the core reaction (restriction field). Requires a §bLens§f (high durability, eventually needs replacement). Output adjustable §e1-100%§f — higher = stronger restriction but more power and lens wear.

§bFuel Injector§f (hbm:dfc_injector) — supplies 2 fuel types to the core. No fluid container slots — pipe fuel in directly. Fluid identifiers auto-update the core's fuel types. Does NOT require power.

§bTungsten Crate§f — alternative to Receiver. Uses beam heat for industrial processing: instant smelting, crucible charging, and polonium-210 → §bYharonite§f transmutation.

§6Safety§f:
§b• Always have stabilizers online
§b• Use §e2§f stabilizers for redundancy
§b• Install a §bHigh Energy Field Jammer§f in case of emergency
§b• Never stand in line-of-sight of the beam

§eTip§f: Design your DFC layout carefully before placing blocks. The 15-block max range gives flexibility for compact designs. A 3×3 column setup is possible for the brave!
